Layer 0 durable submit: reserve-only submit, DB-enforced active idempotency, recovery-point phases, poll-side resume, and stranded reservation recovery.

claims: PRD is a sound, well-formed product contract
Each PRD promise maps to a realizable, existing source boundary
Out-of-scope (SQLite-only, no Postgres/lease/reviewer-panel) is respected by the diff

criteria: PRD doc read in full and is well-formed
P1-P5 each map to a real current-source boundary
evidence: tests/test_dual_agent_workflow_driver.py (submit->popen==[]/no-request/pid-None then poll spawns; 8-proc race; stranded->terminal)

payload.content: prd_review ACCEPT. PRD out-of-scope forbids Postgres/lease -> diff is SQLite-only, no such code — no contradiction

planning-artifact sha256 not re-hashed (approval declined per policy)

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Finding a Popen or request-file write inside the submit body, a duplicate non-partial idempotency index, or out-of-scope (Postgres/lease/reviewer-panel) code in the diff.

Layer 0 durable submit: reserve-only submit, DB-enforced active idempotency, recovery-point phases, poll-side resume, and stranded reservation recovery.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

tests non-vacuous and cover each invariant
